> *Your three-month average was **$8,003 per month**, including April’s annual property-tax payment.*

Using the three complete months **April–June 2026**, your true spending was:

| Month | Checking expenses | Card purchases, net of refunds | True spending |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| April | $9,307.69 | $896.03 | **$10,203.72** |

April included a **$3,850 property-tax payment**. Spreading that irregular expense aside, your recurring-style spending baseline is approximately **$6,720 per month**.

I excluded an average of **$2,235.68 per month** in non-spending cash movements:

- Transfers to brokerage and 529 accounts: **$1,200.00/month**
- Credit-card payments from checking: **$1,035.68/month**

The card purchases themselves remain included once—when the purchases occurred—so they aren’t lost or double-counted.
